name: Camera
description: DSLR cameras, mirrorless cameras, action cameras (GoPro), film cameras, and their accessories. Camera equipment is generally permitted on flights with some considerations.
tsaGuideline: Camera equipment including DSLRs, mirrorless, and action cameras are allowed through TSA security. Camera lenses and bodies may be subject to additional screening. X-ray machines are generally safe for digital cameras but film should be kept in carry-on.
iataGuideline: Camera equipment is permitted in both cabin and hold baggage. Film and spare lithium batteries must be in the cabin.
capacityLimit: No specific capacity limit, subject to carry-on bag size limits
restrictions: Camera batteries (lithium) must be in carry-on if spare/external
Undeveloped film: request hand inspection — X-ray may damage film above 800 ISO
Camera equipment counts toward your carry-on allowance
Very large professional camera setups may require special handling

airlineRules.notes: Camera equipment allowed. Spare batteries must be in carry-on.
Cameras and spare batteries in cabin baggage only for spare batteries.

faqs.question: Can I bring a camera on a plane?
faqs.answer: Yes, cameras are allowed on planes in both carry-on and checked baggage. However, carrying your camera in your carry-on is strongly recommended to avoid damage or theft from the cargo hold.

faqs.question: Will airport X-ray machines damage my camera?
faqs.answer: Modern digital camera sensors are not affected by airport X-ray machines. However, undeveloped photographic film can be damaged, especially film above ISO 800. Request a hand inspection for film to be safe.

faqs.question: Can I bring extra camera batteries on a plane?
faqs.answer: Spare (loose) lithium batteries including camera batteries must be in your carry-on bag — they cannot be in checked luggage. Each spare battery must be individually protected to prevent short circuits.
